name: MASSAMAN CURRY
description: This is an absolute jaw-dropper of a curry. It has an incredible depth of flavour, aromatic richness and richness that keeps on giving. The spice kick is big but not too bad. A gorgeous dish throughout the dish. You could make this super-authentic and leave it in the slow cooker all day for melt-in-your-mouth veggies. Serve with Perfectly Boiled Rice (see page 20).
method: Large saucepan on a high heat | Liquidiser
Put the fennel, cumin, coriander, lemongrass and cloves into the saucepan and toast for about 2 minutes, until fragrant and aromatic | Put the mixture into the liquidiser and set aside

Trim the top and bottom off the lemongrass and carefully cut them in half lengthways | Peel the shallots and garlic | Peel the ginger, scraping off the skin with a spoon | Chop the leaves from the fresh coriander and set aside | Coriander stalks go into the paste

Roughly chop the lemongrass, shallots, garlic and ginger and tip them into the pan | Fry for 5 minutes, until lightly browned, then tip into the liquidiser with the toasted spices and half the coriander stalks | Blend until you've created a completely smooth paste with a little oil from the pan and no bits | Blitz for 2-3 minutes, making sure you can no longer see any lumpy bits for 2 minutes, or until golden brown | Pour in the coconut milk and stir to make the liquid a lovely, creamy colour | To a gentle boil

Meanwhile, peel the potato and sweet potatoes and chop into 3cm cubes | Cut the pepper in half and cut out the stem and seeds, then chop into 3cm cubes along with the green beans | Break the cauliflower into small florets

Add the vegetable stock to the pan, followed by the potato, sweet potato, cauliflower, red pepper and green beans | Add the tamarind paste, bay leaves and cinnamon stick and bring to the boil, stirring continuously | Immediately reduce the heat to low and leave to simmer for 40 minutes, until all the vegetables are tender, stirring occasionally for a rich, curry consistency
Chop the reserved coriander leaves | Serve the curry alongside boiled rice, scattered with the toasted peanuts and fresh coriander
